title: A Very Polite Coup
description: The generals and ministers replaced you during lunch. The paperwork was already prepared.

title: People Had Enough
description: The people stormed the palace, mostly because it had the only working air conditioner.

title: Nationwide Shrug
description: Order collapsed completely. The last traffic sign was traded for a sandwich.

title: Officially Broke
description: The treasury is empty. Creditors seized the palace furniture, including your favorite chair.

title: Nation in Darkness
description: The blackout outlasted your patience. Citizens learned to navigate by rumor and candlelight. The palace remained visible from space.

title: The Purrfect Transfer of Power
description: Parliament voted unanimously to replace you with a cat. The cat immediately declared a nap.

title: Still Standing, Somehow
description: Thirty days in power and the country still exists. Historians will call this period 'eventful'.

title: An Unexpected Peace
description: The ministers ran out of ideas. For the first time in history, the country experienced peace.

title: The Eternal Smile Republic
description: Frowning was classified as treason. Historians agree everyone looked very committed.

title: The Parade Republic
description: General Boom seized power during a marching rehearsal. The coup lasted four minutes but was very synchronized.

title: The National Mascot General
description: General Boom now rides a parade float shaped like himself. Authority remains ceremonial. The salute is mandatory.

title: The Loyal Protector
description: General Boom never seized the palace. He only seized every parade schedule until the end of time.

title: The Brighter Moon Decree
description: Ministan replaced the moon with a more efficient model. Tides filed a complaint.

title: The Experimental Republic
description: Every law became a trial and every trial became a law. Historians request safety goggles.

title: The Experiment Leaves
description: Science packed politely and took a taxi. Clerk Zero stamped EXIT. The beakers waved.

title: The Country Is Acquired
description: Ministan became a subsidiary with a flag. Letterhead changed. Historians list shareholders first.

title: Profit Buys Retirement
description: Sir Profit retired on a yacht named Alignment. Public signs returned. The invoice was enormous.

title: The AI Accepts Your Resignation
description: A polite email ended the term. Subject line: Optimization complete.

title: The Moon Has a New Owner
description: Title transferred. Night remained free to look at. Ownership was another matter.

title: Corporate Ministan, LLC
description: Public benches became brand real estate. Historians list the country as a sitting subscription with a flag.

title: Scientific Golden Age (Preliminary)
description: The artificial sun stayed up. Historians call it a golden age with invoices attached and nights marked optional.

title: Smallest Superpower
description: Ministan signed a volume treaty and called it soft power. Historians list the country as a tiny superpower of negotiated noise.

title: Tanks Direct Everything
description: Parking, traffic, and policy answered to tread marks. Historians underline the civic square in armor.

title: Traffic Achieves Peace
description: Ministan solved gridlock with schedules, flowers, and one very polite roundabout. Historians call it the Quiet Commute Accords.

title: The Balanced Miracle
description: Minister Penny balanced the final budget without deleting the citizens. Historians still check the footnotes twice.

title: The Spreadsheet State
description: The ledger became a religion. Citizens prayed in columns. Happiness was a rounding error.

title: Austerity Without Citizens
description: The books were perfect. The streets were empty. Minister Penny filed a victory form.

title: Everyone Is a Millionaire
description: Ministan printed until every citizen was rich and broke. Wheelbarrows became wallets. Historians call it festive arithmetic.

title: Government by Form
description: Ministan ran on Form F-0 and a paid efficiency miracle. Historians list the country as a filing cabinet with a flag.

title: The Broadcast State
description: Ministan became a continuous special report. Citizens lived between commercial breaks. Historians still wait for the credits.

title: The Day Everyone Stopped Believing
description: Official information became optional. Screens went politely blank. The nation shrugged in stereo.

title: Olga's People's Cabinet
description: Kitchen-table petitions became ministries. Auntie Olga brought thermos flasks to cabinet. The palace learned queue etiquette.

title: The Palace Hears the Street
description: The ruler listened. The street answered with sandwiches and schedules. Reform arrived wearing sensible shoes.

title: Olga Sends You Home
description: Auntie Olga refused the crown and suggested you try living like a neighbor. The movement stayed civic. The palace felt suddenly quiet.

title: Happiness Reaches 100%
description: The National Happiness Index froze at perfection. Streets remained complicated. Historians filed it under measured triumph.

title: Peaceful Accidental Democracy
description: A ceremonial form accidentally counted real preferences. Ministan celebrated with orderly queues and surprising ballots.

title: Democracy by Administrative Error
description: A void stamp arrived one day late. By then the tallies had become government. Clerk Zero filed it under unintended consequences.

title: The Purrfect Transfer
description: Whiskers completed a solemn transfer of symbolic power. You became Chief Litter Officer. The nation napped.

title: The Boxes Truce
description: The cat revolution ended in cardboard. Politics returned to human absurdity with better blankets.

title: The Final Stamp
description: One last stamp descended on Ministan. Forms governed forms. Historians requested larger cabinets.

title: Clerk Zero Closes the File
description: Clerk Zero closed the national folder. Dust settled. A blank form remained as a souvenir.

title: Eternal National Festival
description: The credits never rolled. Confetti became weather. Sleep became optional.

title: Beloved Retirement
description: You canceled the eternal parade. Citizens gifted a quiet chair. It was enough.

title: The Great Cheese Settlement
description: Border toast resumed. Tinyria complimented Ministan's maturity. Historians filed dairy diplomacy.

title: Palace Beautiful and Empty
description: The ballroom won awards. Nobody lived there fully. Beauty filed for divorce from use.

title: Renovation Reveals the Truth
description: Ledgers went on display. Scaffolding became a museum. The country learned what gold cost.
